questions.question_text: Patient Name:
Date of Birth:
Address:
Tel No:
Can the patient understand and communicate at an acceptable level? Do you need to undertake a mental capacity assessment? Does the patient have an LPA in place?

Does the medical history indicate any potential problems? (Consider the patient's health and the possibility or likelihood of collapse or another medical emergency such as cardiac or respiratory disease, epilepsy, dementia or other conditions which can impact swallowing)
Are there any special risks arising from the treatment to be provided?
Is there an appropriate level of social support and aftercare if treatment is to be provided at home?
Details of others present, e.g. carer, relative, support worker, etc.
External Access - Findings at the premises
External Lighting - Findings at the premises
Internal Access - Findings at the premises
Internal Lighting - Findings at the premises
Obvious Fire Hazards - Findings at the premises
Slips, trips and falls. Any items that have a potential to cause slips, trips or falls - Findings at the premises
Electrical Safety - Findings at the premises
Other Hazards e.g. Animals - Findings at the premises
Furniture - Findings at the premises
Space Availability - Findings at the premises

Additional Comments (Any other information that is relative to the risk assessment in question. It is important that this information is as accurate as possible. Although assessments can be subjective, hearsay should not be used as a basis of assumption)
Is the physical environment safe for the intended procedure?
Overall Risk Identified as:
Based on the above risk assessment, should emergency drugs and equipment be taken on the visit?
